task_completion.description: Access the FreeMED clinical template creation interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeMED application is open in a web browser and the screen for creating or editing a clinical text template is visible.
The text 'Normal Physical Exam' is entered into the template name or title input field.

task_completion.description: Enter the General and HEENT sections into the template body
task_completion.visual_evidence: The template body text area contains 'General: Well developed, well nourished, no acute distress.' and 'HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA.'

task_completion.description: Enter the Cardiovascular and Lungs sections into the template body
task_completion.visual_evidence: The template body text area contains 'Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.' and 'Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.'

task_completion.description: Enter the Abdomen and Extremities sections into the template body
task_completion.visual_evidence: The template body text area contains 'Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended.' and 'Extremities: No edema or cyanosis.'

task_completion.description: Save the template to the system
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the template was saved, or the 'Normal Physical Exam' template is visible in the system's list/directory of saved templates.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeMED web interface to create the template
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the FreeMED GUI in a web browser, rather than terminal commands, API calls, or database scripts.

integrity.description: The agent entered the template into FreeMED, not a generic text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The text is being typed into the FreeMED web application form fields, not a local application like Notepad, Gedit, or Nano.

integrity.description: The text is genuinely entered into the application form
integrity.visual_evidence: The required text is located inside the actual input fields of the FreeMED template form, not just visible on the screen in a task description window or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a clinical template titled 'Normal Physical Exam'. The template body must include the HEENT text 'Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA' and the cardiovascular text 'no murmurs, rubs, or gallops'.
